8	def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
9	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
10	        prog="bookmark",
11	        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
12	    )
13	    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")
14	
15	    # --- add ---
16	    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
17	    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
18	    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
19	    add_parser.add_argument(
20	        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
21	    )
22	    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")
23	
24	    # --- list ---
25	    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
26	    list_parser.add_argument(
27	        "--format",
28	        choices=["table", "json"],
29	        default="table",
30	        help="Output format (default: table)",
31	    )
32	
33	    # --- delete ---
34	    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
35	    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")
36	
37	    # --- show ---
38	    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
39	    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")
40	
41	    return parser
42	
43	
44	def main(argv=None):
45	    parser = build_parser()
46	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
47	
48	    if args.command is None:
49	        parser.print_help()
50	        sys.exit(1)
51	
52	    if args.command == "add":
53	        bookmark = add_bookmark(
54	            url=args.url,
55	            title=args.title,
56	            tags=args.tags,
57	            notes=args.notes,
58	        )
59	        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")
60	
61	    elif args.command == "list":
62	        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
63	        if args.format == "json":
64	            print(format_json(bookmarks))
65	        else:

67

7	def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
8	    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""

10	        return "No bookmarks found."

12	    # Column widths

23	    )
24	    separator = "-" * len(header)
25	    lines = [header, separator]
26	
27	    for b in bookmarks:
28	        short_id = b.id[:8]
29	        title = b.title[:title_w]
30	        url = b.url[:url_w]
31	        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]

33	            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
34	            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
35	            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
36	            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
37	        )
38	
39	    return "\n".join(lines)

42	def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:

11	def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:

27	def add_bookmark(
28	    url: str,
29	    title: str,
30	    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
31	    notes: str = "",
32	    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
33	) -> Bookmark:
34	    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""

37	        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
38	        url=url,
39	        title=title,

41	        notes=notes,
42	    )

45	    return bookmark

8	def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
9	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
10	        prog="bookmark",
11	        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
12	    )
13	    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")
14	
15	    # --- add ---
16	    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
17	    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
18	    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
19	    add_parser.add_argument(
20	        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
21	    )
22	    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")
23	
24	    # --- list ---
25	    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
26	    list_parser.add_argument(
27	        "--format",
28	        choices=["table", "json"],
29	        default="table",
30	        help="Output format (default: table)",
31	    )
32	
33	    # --- delete ---
34	    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
35	    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")
36	
37	    # --- show ---
38	    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
39	    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")
40	
41	    return parser
42	
43	
44	def main(argv=None):
45	    parser = build_parser()
46	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
47	
48	    if args.command is None:
49	        parser.print_help()
50	        sys.exit(1)
51	
52	    if args.command == "add":
53	        bookmark = add_bookmark(
54	            url=args.url,
55	            title=args.title,
56	            tags=args.tags,
57	            notes=args.notes,
58	        )
59	        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")
60	
61	    elif args.command == "list":
62	        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
63	        if args.format == "json":
64	            print(format_json(bookmarks))
65	        else:
66	            print(format_table(bookmarks))
67	
68	    elif args.command == "delete":

71	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
72	        else:
73	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import json
from typing import List

from .models import Bookmark


def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
    if not bookmarks:
        return "No bookmarks found."

    # Column widths
    id_w = 8
    title_w = 30
    url_w = 40
    tags_w = 20

    header = (
        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
    )
    separator = "-" * len(header)
    lines = [header, separator]

    for b in bookmarks:
        short_id = b.id[:8]
        title = b.title[:title_w]
        url = b.url[:url_w]
        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
        lines.append(
            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
        )

    return "\n".join(lines)


def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

# Plan: Add `search` Command to Bookmark CLI

## Context

The bookmark CLI (`src/cli.py`) currently supports `add`, `list`, `delete`, and `show` commands but has no way to filter bookmarks. A test file (`tests/test_search.py`) already exists and fully specifies the expected behavior — it imports from `src.search` (which does not exist yet) and tests both the search logic functions and CLI integration. The goal is to implement everything those tests require so that all tests pass.

---

## What Needs to Be Done

Two files need to be changed/created:

1. **CREATE `src/search.py`** — the search logic module
2. **EDIT `src/cli.py`** — wire in the `search` subcommand

---

## Step-by-Step Implementation

### Step 1 — Create `src/search.py`

Create `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775463528433-l09i72/src/search.py`

Implement four functions:

#### `search_by_title(bookmarks, query) -> List[Bookmark]`
- Case-insensitive substring match against `bookmark.title`
- Implementation: `query.lower() in bookmark.title.lower()`

#### `search_by_tag(bookmarks, tag) -> List[Bookmark]`
- Exact match — the tag string must be present in `bookmark.tags` list
- Implementation: `tag in bookmark.tags`

#### `search_by_domain(bookmarks, domain) -> List[Bookmark]`
- Extract the hostname from `bookmark.url` using `urllib.parse.urlparse(url).netloc`
- Match if the bookmark's netloc equals the query domain OR ends with `"." + domain`
  - `netloc == domain` handles exact domain match (e.g., `"github.com"`)
  - `netloc.endswith("." + domain)` handles subdomain match (e.g., `"docs.python.org"` when querying `"docs.python.org"` — actually this IS an exact match, but the test `test_matches_domain` with `"github.com"` expects 2 results: `github.com/runpod/...` and `github.com/pallets/flask`, both have netloc exactly `"github.com"`)
  - **Key insight from tests**: `search_by_domain(bookmarks, "docs.python.org")` should return 2 results — both bookmarks whose URL netloc is `docs.python.org`. This is a simple exact netloc match. No subdomain wildcard logic needed beyond that.
  - Actually re-reading: the test name is `test_matches_subdomain` and queries `"docs.python.org"` expecting 2 results. Both URLs are `https://docs.python.org/3/...`, so netloc == `"docs.python.org"` for both. So the domain matching is just: `netloc == domain` (exact hostname match).

#### `search(bookmarks, title=None, tag=None, domain=None) -> List[Bookmark]`
- If no criteria provided (`all None`), return all bookmarks unchanged
- Otherwise, start with the full list and apply each non-None criterion with AND logic (successive filtering):
  ```python
  result = bookmarks
  if title is not None:
      result = search_by_title(result, title)
  if tag is not None:
      result = search_by_tag(result, tag)
  if domain is not None:
      result = search_by_domain(result, domain)
  return result
  ```

**Full file content:**
```python
"""Search functionality for bookmarks."""

from typing import List, Optional
from urllib.parse import urlparse

from .models import Bookmark


def search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks whose title contains query (case-insensitive)."""
    q = query.lower()
    return [b for b in bookmarks if q in b.title.lower()]


def search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks that have an exact tag match."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if tag in b.tags]


def search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks whose URL hostname matches the given domain."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if urlparse(b.url).netloc == domain]


def search(
    bookmarks: List[Bookmark],
    title: Optional[str] = None,
    tag: Optional[str] = None,
    domain: Optional[str] = None,
) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Search bookmarks using AND logic across all provided criteria."""
    result = bookmarks
    if title is not None:
        result = search_by_title(result, title)
    if tag is not None:
        result = search_by_tag(result, tag)
    if domain is not None:
        result = search_by_domain(result, domain)
    return result
```

---

### Step 2 — Edit `src/cli.py`

File: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775463528433-l09i72/src/cli.py`

**Change 1 — Add import at the top** (after the existing imports):
```python
from .search import search
```

**Change 2 — Add `search` subparser** inside `build_parser()`, after the `show` subparser (before `return parser`):
```python
# --- search ---
search_parser = subparsers.add_parser("search", help="Search bookmarks")
search_parser.add_argument("--title", default=None, help="Filter by title substring")
search_parser.add_argument("--tag", default=None, help="Filter by exact tag")
search_parser.add_argument("--domain", default=None, help="Filter by domain")
search_parser.add_argument(
    "--format",
    choices=["table", "json"],
    default="table",
    help="Output format (default: table)",
)
```

**Change 3 — Add `search` command handler** inside `main()`, after the `show` block:
```python
elif args.command == "search":
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
    results = search(bookmarks, title=args.title, tag=args.tag, domain=args.domain)
    if args.format == "json":
        print(format_json(results))
    else:
        print(format_table(results))
```

---

## Critical Files

| File | Action | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| `src/search.py` | **CREATE** | All four search functions |
| `src/cli.py` | **EDIT** | Import search, add subparser, add handler |
| `tests/test_search.py` | Read-only reference | 180-line test file that drives implementation |

## Reuse of Existing Code

- **`format_table(bookmarks)`** from `src/formatter.py` — reused as-is for table output
- **`format_json(bookmarks)`** from `src/formatter.py` — reused as-is for JSON output
- **`load_bookmarks()`** from `src/storage.py` — reused as-is to load all bookmarks before filtering
- **`Bookmark` dataclass** from `src/models.py` — used as the type for all search inputs/outputs

No new formatting or storage logic needed.

---

## Verification

Run the test suite:
```bash

python -m pytest tests/ -v
```

```python
"""Search functionality for bookmarks."""

from typing import List, Optional
from urllib.parse import urlparse

from .models import Bookmark


def search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks whose title contains query (case-insensitive)."""
    q = query.lower()
    return [b for b in bookmarks if q in b.title.lower()]


def search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks that have an exact tag match."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if tag in b.tags]


def search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks whose URL hostname matches the given domain."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if urlparse(b.url).netloc == domain]


def search(
    bookmarks: List[Bookmark],
    title: Optional[str] = None,
    tag: Optional[str] = None,
    domain: Optional[str] = None,
) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Search bookmarks using AND logic across all provided criteria.
```

    Returns all bookmarks when no criteria are given.
    """
    result = bookmarks
    if title is not None:
        result = search_by_title(result, title)
    if tag is not None:
        result = search_by_tag(result, tag)
    if domain is not None:
        result = search_by_domain(result, domain)
    return result

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
